reforms.description: The Chilean government is pursuing a 'Pro-growth Agenda' aimed at enhancing international competitiveness through various policy reforms, including the promotion of a venture capital industry via guarantees for venture capital funds and subsidies for seed capital to small firms and enterprise incubators, thereby supporting innovative ventures and enhancing the startup ecosystem.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance the startup ecosystem and support innovative ventures, which are expected to drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The subsidies for seed capital and the venture capital guarantees have been put into practice.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant structural change in supporting the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Further measures to deepen financial markets are currently being considered in a second wave of capital market reforms... including the creation of incentives to develop a venture capital industry.

reforms.description: The Chilean government proposed the creation of instruments and initiatives to promote private R&D, aiming to enhance the national innovation system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing private R&D is expected to foster innovation and competitiveness in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal has been officially announced but not yet legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposal represents a significant but narrower reform aimed at improving the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The government aims to improve the national innovation system: creation of instruments and initiatives to promote private R&D.

reforms.description: The Chilean government supports research and technological development through CORFO and CONICYT, which manage various funding programs aimed at enhancing scientific and technological output.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Public funding for research and technological development is crucial for fostering innovation and scientific advancement.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding programs managed by CORFO and CONICYT are currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major structural change in public funding for R&D.
reforms.source_quote: The Chilean government does not offer tax incentives to R&D but supports research and technological development mainly through CORFO and the National Commission for Scientific and Technological Research (CONICYT).

reforms.description: The Chilean government is focusing on improving linkages between the scientific community and industry to enhance the responsiveness of the science system to economic opportunities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving linkages between science and industry is expected to drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives to improve linkages are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant effort to enhance the innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The current reorientation of science and technology policy, with a greater emphasis on measures that strengthen linkages between the Chilean scientific community, industry and public-sector users of research findings, is a necessary initial step.

reforms.description: A programme was launched to develop suppliers in the mining sector, providing subsidies to large firms that train and coordinate local suppliers, enhancing their qualifications and capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing local suppliers' capabilities is expected to improve the overall competitiveness of the mining sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The supplier development programme has been operational since its launch.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant but narrower reform focused on supplier development.
reforms.source_quote: To assist in diversifying the customer base, different agencies and business representatives have also worked together to upgrade the qualifications of small suppliers.

reforms.description: A tax exemption for training has been initiated to encourage firms to invest in the development of their workforce, particularly in the mining sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging workforce development is expected to enhance productivity and innovation in the mining sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax exemption has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The tax exemption represents a significant but narrower reform aimed at workforce development.
reforms.source_quote: Finally, tax exemption for training has been initiated.
